chunk_summary: Strategies to minimize feed interruptions and audit delivered volume are essential for optimizing enteral nutrition in pediatric critical care, especially during procedures and imaging.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of minimizing enteral nutrition interruptions in pediatric critical care settings.
Learn strategies for coordinating feed schedules with procedures to reduce fasting periods.
Implement delivered-versus-prescribed audits to monitor nutrition delivery and address deficiencies.
Explore methods to safely resume feeding after brief interruptions, including postpyloric feeding protocols.
key_facts: Nutrition interruptions are a major cause of reduced enteral nutrition delivery in critically ill children.
Delivered volume <80% of prescription for 24–48 hours should be treated as clinical deterioration in nutrition delivery.
Continuous feeding regimens are recommended when frequent interruptions are predictable.
Postpyloric feeding may allow shorter holds compared to gastric feeding for certain procedures.
In resource-limited settings, delivered-versus-prescribed audits are a high-yield intervention to improve nutrition delivery.
reference_quote: Guidelines for the provision and assessment of nutrition support therapy in the pediatric critically ill patient (ASPEN/SCCM 2017) emphasize the importance of minimizing fasting and optimizing feed delivery during routine care and procedures.
document_key_topics: Delivered volume audits in enteral nutrition
Continuous feeding regimens in critical care
